title: eupatorium betonicifolium var. integrifolium
intro: Eupatorium betonicifolium var. integrifolium, also known as Eupatorium, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of Central and South America, characterized by its upright stems and green, elliptical leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Eupatorium include the relief of fever, pain, and inflammation, as well as the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Eupatorium can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions.
